title: Snowflake MVP: Connection, Authentication, and Schema Browser
story: As a data analyst, I want to connect my Snowflake account to DataTools Pro and browse my databases, schemas, and table structures so that I can use my Snowflake metadata for data migration and mapping activities.
description:
Build the Snowflake MVP integration enabling users to connect their Snowflake account to DataTools Pro.

Connection setup requires:
- Account identifier
- User credentials
- Warehouse
- Role
- Database

Once connected, users can:
1. View a list of databases from their Snowflake account
2. Select a database to see its schemas
3. Select a schema to see its tables
4. Select a table to view its field definitions

The integration uses Snowflake's OAuth 2.0 flow for authentication, storing access tokens and refresh tokens per team. A settings page includes an expandable accordion with instructions for generating Snowflake client ID and secret.

The Snowflake REST API is used for fetching database metadata.
acceptance_criteria: User can authenticate with Snowflake using OAuth 2.0
Access token and refresh token are securely stored per team
User can view a list of Snowflake databases
User can drill into a database to see its schemas
User can drill into a schema to see its tables
User can drill into a table to view field definitions
Settings page includes instructions for generating Snowflake credentials
Token refresh is handled when tokens expire
ux_changes: New Snowflake settings page with OAuth connection flow and credential setup instructions in an expandable accordion. Schema browser UI showing a drill-down hierarchy: databases > schemas > tables > fields.
middleware_changes: Snowflake OAuth 2.0 client with token exchange, storage, and refresh. Service layer for fetching database metadata (databases, schemas, tables, fields) via the Snowflake REST API.
data_model_changes: New table for Snowflake OAuth tokens (access token, refresh token) with a one-to-one relationship to the team/account.
api_changes: OAuth callback endpoint for Snowflake authorization. Outbound calls to Snowflake REST API for database/schema/table metadata retrieval.
implementation_notes: Uses the Snowflake REST API rather than the PHP PDO driver. OAuth tokens are stored per-team. Token refresh was working on localhost but had issues on the development server that required debugging. A dedicated subdomain (snowflake.datatoolspro.com) was set up as a CNAME for the callback URL, but this caused auth middleware issues since the user session is on app.datatoolspro.com, not the snowflake subdomain.
comment_summary: Developer built the OAuth flow and schema browser. Token refresh worked locally but had deployment issues. A callback URL challenge emerged: using snowflake.datatoolspro.com as the callback domain caused auth middleware to redirect users to login since the session was scoped to app.datatoolspro.com. This cross-domain session issue was still being resolved at the time of review.
